name: toggle-group
description: A set of toggle buttons for single or multiple selection

meta.name: ToggleGroup
meta.description: A set of toggle buttons for single or multiple selection

props.name: type
props.description: Whether one or multiple items can be pressed at once ('single' | 'multiple').

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Called with the new value when it changes.

props.name: items
props.type: { value: string; label?: string; icon?: React.ReactNode; disabled?: boolean }[]

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disables the control and removes it from the tab order.

examples.title: Single selection
examples.code: <ToggleGroup type="single" defaultValue="left" items={[{ value: "left", label: "Left" }, { value: "center", label: "Center" }, { value: "right", label: "Right" }]} />

examples.title: Multiple selection
examples.code: <ToggleGroup type="multiple" defaultValue={["bold"]} items={[{ value: "bold", label: "Bold" }, { value: "italic", label: "Italic" }]} />

whenToUse: Choosing one value from a small, mutually exclusive set that should stay visible (type="single")
Toggling several independent options in a compact segmented control (type="multiple")
whenNotToUse: The options are independent actions, not a selection — use ButtonGroup
There are many options or they need labels and descriptions — use Radio or Checkbox groups

antiPatterns.why: Single-select used as a controlled value should be driven by value/onValueChange so the source of truth lives with the parent

related.name: ButtonGroup
related.reason: Use when the buttons fire independent actions rather than select a value

related.name: Toggle
related.reason: Use a single Toggle for one standalone binary option

intent.a11yRationale: Single mode renders role="radiogroup" with role="radio" + aria-checked items; multiple mode uses a plain group of buttons with aria-pressed. Roving tabindex makes the set one tab stop and arrow keys move between items, matching the APG radiogroup/toolbar patterns

content.tone: Short noun or icon per option
content.notes: Keep item labels parallel and concise; provide a label or icon meaningful out of context

flexibility.note: Item height must resolve to --cascivo-control-height-* to align with other controls
Items may use a text label, an icon, or both; consumer owns the icon set
